title: alnus viridis var. fruticosa
intro: Alnus viridis var. fruticosa, also known as the green alder or mountain alder, is a variety of the green alder tree that is native to Europe and Asia. It is a deciduous shrub or small tree with medicinal properties, particularly in its bark, leaves, and cones. The bark and leaves of the Alnus viridis var. fruticosa contain salicylic acid, a compound with anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can be used to treat various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and wounds. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, or used in tinctures and salves, offering potential health benefits including reduced inflammation, improved wound healing, and relief from pain and fever.
